methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series A → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.0x) to $12M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 7 same-sector + same/adjacent-stage peers in Robotics & Manufacturing. Range: $300M - $10.0B.

private.name: Foundation Robotics
private.rationale: Same sector (Robotics & Manufacturing), same stage (Series A), post-money $3B+ (reported, raise in progress)

data.narrative:
Salient Motion is a Series A robotics and manufacturing company that has raised $12M to date. Valuation analysis draws on a comparison universe of nine private robotics firms and one public comparable (ticker 277810.KQ), spanning stages from Series A through later rounds.

Two triangulation methods were applied. Stage-progression analysis, using total raised as a proxy for current valuation and applying a 2.0x step-up multiplier typical of Series A advancement, implies a $24M valuation. Peer-median analysis across seven same-sector, same/adjacent-stage comparables—including Foundation Robotics ($3.0B), 1X Technologies ($10.0B), Bedrock Robotics ($1.8B), Dexterity ($1.6B), and Collaborative Robotics ($500M)—yields a median valuation of $1.6B, with a range of $300M to $10.0B.

The two methods produce a wide spread, reflecting the heterogeneity of robotics valuations at this stage. Triangulated analysis supports a range of $20M (floor) to $1.9B (ceiling), with a median of $837M. The substantial gap between stage-progression ($24M) and peer-median ($1.6B) reflects uncertainty around Salient Motion's relative competitive position and unit economics within a sector marked by high variance in funding outcomes. Key downside drivers include execution risk and capital intensity; upside depends on market traction and differentiation versus the sampled peer set.
